stemHtml: A flight instructor is demonstrating stall and stall recovery during a lesson. As a part of the exercise, the instructor demonstrates the use of excessive pitch-down stick input during the initial portion of the recovery. Recovering from the dive, the pilot applies excessive back force on the stick, which forces the aeroplane to enter a secondary (accelerated) stall. What is the correct procedure for handling the aircraft after entering a demonstrated spiral dive?

options.a: The aircraft will suffer deformation when recovering.
options.c: Pull back the controls but the plane will suffer deformation.
